title: Launch an Internal AI Knowledge-Sharing Channel for Your Finance Team
description: Create a team-wide AI sharing loop where one person's win becomes everyone's accelerator — with real examples of what finance teams have built.

steps.title: Create a dedicated AI sharing channel
steps.content: Set up a Slack/Teams channel specifically for sharing AI wins. Not a general "tech" channel — a focused space where people post what they built and how.

steps.title: Seed it with your first build
steps.content:
Don't wait for others. Post your own AI win first with a short description: what the task was, what tool you used, and how much time it saved. Examples:

- A pricing calculator built in one afternoon with Claude (saved 1-2 hours per deal)
- A product roadmap pipeline automated via Google Sheets + script (30-60 min → 2 min)

steps.title: Encourage non-engineers to share
steps.content: The best wins often come from finance, product, and customer success people — not developers. Make it clear: you don't need to code. If you used AI to solve a problem, share it.

steps.title: Highlight compound wins
steps.content: When someone's share inspires a better version from someone else, call it out. The channel works as a compounding loop, not a knowledge base.

steps.title: Document high-impact automations as templates
steps.content:
When a team member builds something that saves significant time, document the prompt/workflow so others can replicate it:

- AR/AP interactive reports from GL data
- QBR operations (scheduling, prep reminders, post-call summaries)
- Auto-generated SOWs from pricing calculators

steps.title: Review and promote the best wins monthly
steps.content: Once a month, highlight the top 3 AI wins from the channel. Share the time saved and encourage the team to build on them.
